name: FactSet OnDemand Field Dictionary (from Web Services Reference Manual v2.0.2)
description: Field-code dictionary extracted from the official 'FactSet OnDemand - Web Services Reference Manual' (doc v2.0.2). IMPORTANT: that manual documents the OnDemand WEB SERVICES (HTTPS API / Factlets such as ExtractDataSnapshot, EstimatesOnDemand, CorporateActionsDividends). This app does NOT call that HTTP API - it generates Excel =FDS(ticker,"FIELD(...)") formulas. However, the web API and the Excel =FDS() add-in share the same underlying FactSet languages (FQL/FSL): the same FIELD CODES and the same DATE CONVENTIONS. So the field codes below are generally usable in =FDS() too, SUBJECT TO your FactSet entitlements. Each entry carries an `fds_compatible` flag and a note. Fields whose exact argument signature was not explicit in the manual are flagged 'verify arg signature / entitlement in FactSet' rather than guessed. Estimates (FE_*) are captured in depth because they back the app's Phase C data generator. SYNTAX NOTE: the Excel =FDS() field form uses COMMA-separated date args INSIDE the field parens, most-recent-first, e.g. P_PRICE(0D,-250D,D). The web service instead uses a separate &dates=start:end colon argument - do not mix the two.
source: FactSet OnDemand - Web Services Reference Manual, doc version 2.0.2 (FactSet Research Systems Inc., 2021)

price.label: Daily closing price
price.notes: Closing price. Time series via comma-separated, most-recent-first date args inside the parens, e.g. P_PRICE(0D,-250D,D); single point-in-time via P_PRICE(0D). Most frequently used field in the manual's examples.

volume.label: Daily volume
volume.notes: Daily share volume. The manual's examples reference P_VOLUME; in the Excel add-in P_VOLUME_DAY is the preferred daily-volume field (P_VOLUME is the legacy name).

ff_sales.label: Sales / revenue (fundamental)
ff_sales.notes: FactSet Fundamentals sales/revenue line item (appears repeatedly in the manual's FF_ examples). {period} uses the FactSet fiscal-period syntax (e.g. a fiscal year like 2024, a relative period, or a quarter like 2024/1F). Verify exact arg signature / entitlement in FactSet.

ff_eps.label: EPS (fundamental, reported)
ff_eps.notes: FactSet Fundamentals reported EPS. {period} = FactSet fiscal-period syntax. Distinct from estimate EPS (use the FE_* estimates fields for forward/consensus EPS). Verify arg signature / entitlement.

fg_eps.label: EPS (FactSet global, point-in-time)
fg_eps.notes: FactSet global EPS field referenced in the manual. {asof} optional point-in-time/period arg. Verify arg signature / entitlement.

fg_price.label: Price (FactSet global)
fg_price.notes: FactSet global price field referenced in the manual. Verify arg signature / entitlement.

fe_item.label: Estimates item selector (e.g. EPS, SALES)
fe_item.notes: Manual: 'The estimates item that is being retrieved, i.e. EPS.' This selects WHICH estimate metric (EPS, SALES, etc.) the surrounding EstimatesOnDemand request returns. In FQL the estimate item is typically supplied as an argument to the FE_* estimate function rather than a standalone field - verify the exact FE_* call form for your entitlement.

fe_per_rel.label: Estimates relative fiscal period (FY1/FY2/NTM)
fe_per_rel.notes: Manual: 'The relative fiscal period specified in the syntax. For example, fiscal period=1 and period type=annual retrieves data for FY1.' This is how forward horizons (FY1, FY2, NTM) are chosen. In =FDS(), the relative period (e.g. 1 = FY1, 2 = FY2) and period type (annual/quarterly) are passed as args to the estimate function - verify the exact signature.

fe_fp_end.label: Estimates fiscal period end date
fe_fp_end.notes: The fiscal-period-end date corresponding to the requested relative period (pairs with FE_PER_REL). Useful for labeling the horizon of a forward estimate.

fe_estimate.label: Detail estimate (broker-level history)
fe_estimate.notes: Manual: 'The detail estimate history from contributing brokers over a specified date range.' Broker-level (not consensus) estimate values; pair with FE_BROKER / FE_ANALYST for attribution and a date range for history.

fe_estimate_value.label: Detailed recommendation / estimate value
fe_estimate_value.notes: Manual references this in the detailed-recommendation context ('the detailed recommendation ... from contributing brokers over a specified range'). Verify exact meaning vs FE_ESTIMATE for your use.

fe_mean.label: Consensus mean estimate
fe_mean.notes: Manual: 'Consensus - Estimate Mean.' The consensus mean for the chosen FE_ITEM and FE_PER_REL (e.g. mean FY1 EPS).

fe_std_dev.label: Consensus estimate standard deviation
fe_std_dev.notes: Manual: 'Consensus - Standard Deviation from Estimate.' Dispersion of analyst estimates - useful as a conviction/uncertainty measure.

fe_num_est.label: Consensus number of estimates
fe_num_est.notes: Manual: 'Consensus - Number of Estimates.' Count of contributing brokers - coverage breadth.

fe_est_rev_val.label: Previous estimate value (for revisions)
fe_est_rev_val.notes: Manual: 'The previous estimate value from the same analyst, for the same fiscal period.' Compare FE_ESTIMATE (current) vs FE_EST_REV_VAL (prior) to measure an earnings REVISION - the core input for an estimate-revision time series.

fe_est_rev_val_arrow.label: Estimate revision direction flag
fe_est_rev_val_arrow.notes: Manual: direction flag for the revision - -1 = latest FE_ESTIMATE is LOWER than FE_EST_REV_VAL (downgrade), 0 = no change, 1 = higher (upgrade). Quick revision-direction signal.

fe_est_rev_val_date.label: Date of previous (revised-from) estimate
fe_est_rev_val_date.notes: Manual: 'The research date of the previous estimate value that corresponds to FE_EST_REV_VAL.' Timestamps the prior estimate in a revision.

fe_mean_date.label: Consensus mean as-of date
fe_mean_date.notes: The date the consensus mean (FE_MEAN) applies to - use to build a dated consensus/revision time series.

fe_actual.label: Reported actual value
fe_actual.notes: Manual: 'The actual value.' The reported actual for the item/period (Actuals report) - pair with consensus for surprise.

fe_actual_flag.label: Actual-available flag
fe_actual_flag.notes: Flag indicating whether an actual has been reported for the period (Actuals report).

fe_report_fy.label: Actual report date / fiscal year
fe_report_fy.notes: Manual: 'The actual report date.' The reporting date/fiscal-year context for the actual.

fe_broker.label: Broker ID (detail estimates)
fe_broker.notes: Contributing broker identifier for detail (broker-level) estimates. Pair with FE_ESTIMATE.

fe_brokername.label: Broker name (detail estimates)
fe_brokername.notes: Contributing broker name for detail estimates.

fe_analyst.label: Analyst ID (detail estimates)
fe_analyst.notes: Contributing analyst identifier for detail estimates.

fe_analystname.label: Analyst name (detail estimates)
fe_analystname.notes: Contributing analyst name for detail estimates.

fe_mark.label: Mean recommendation (numeric)
fe_mark.notes: Manual: 'The mean recommendation.' Numeric consensus recommendation score.

fe_mark_text.label: Mean recommendation (text)
fe_mark_text.notes: Manual: 'The mean recommendation with text string; accessible only with meanText=Y.' Text form of the consensus recommendation (e.g. Buy/Hold).

fe_buy.label: Count of Buy recommendations
fe_buy.notes: Manual: 'The aggregate number of buy recommendations.'

fe_over.label: Count of Overweight recommendations
fe_over.notes: Manual: 'The aggregate number of overweight recommendations.'

fe_hold.label: Count of Hold recommendations
fe_hold.notes: Manual: 'The aggregate number of hold recommendations.'

fe_under.label: Count of Underweight recommendations
fe_under.notes: Manual: 'The aggregate number of underweight recommendations.'

fe_sell.label: Count of Sell recommendations
fe_sell.notes: Manual: 'The aggregate number of sell recommendations.'

fe_no_rec.label: Count of brokers with no recommendation
fe_no_rec.notes: Manual: 'The aggregate number of brokers covering the security that are not providing a recommendation.'

fe_total.label: Number of total estimates revised
fe_total.notes: Manual: 'Consensus - Number of Total Estimates Revised.'

fe_up.label: Count of upward revisions
fe_up.notes: Aggregate count of upward estimate revisions (consensus revision breadth - bullish).

fe_down.label: Count of downward revisions
fe_down.notes: Aggregate count of downward estimate revisions (consensus revision breadth - bearish).

fe_unchanged.label: Count of unchanged estimates
fe_unchanged.notes: Aggregate count of estimates left unchanged in the revision window.

p_opt_close_price.label: Option close price
p_opt_close_price.notes: Closing price of the option (ExtractOptionsSnapshot example). Options data requires the relevant entitlement.

p_opt_exp_date.label: Option expiration date
p_opt_exp_date.notes: Option expiration date (manual shows P_OPT_EXP_DATEN in the options example). Verify exact field spelling for your entitlement.

p_opt_delta.label: Option delta
p_opt_delta.notes: Option delta (greeks) from the options snapshot example.

p_opt_underlying.label: Option underlying security
p_opt_underlying.notes: Underlying security for the option (options snapshot example).

fds_econ_data.label: Economic data item (ExtractEconData)
fds_econ_data.notes: Economic series accessed via the ExtractEconData factlet (items=FDS_ECON_DATA... with an econ identifier). This is primarily a web-service econ mechanism; econ series are not generally pulled through the standard =FDS() security-field form. Treat as reference / verify if your workflow needs econ in Excel.

endpoint_extract_data_snapshot.label: Factlet: ExtractDataSnapshot (web service)
endpoint_extract_data_snapshot.notes: WEB-SERVICE FACTLET (not an =FDS() field). Returns a point-in-time snapshot of one or more items for a universe via HTTPS. The ITEMS it carries (e.g. P_PRICE, FF_*) are the reusable field codes; the factlet itself is the HTTP delivery mechanism.

endpoint_extract_formula_history.label: Factlet: ExtractFormulaHistory (web service)
endpoint_extract_formula_history.notes: WEB-SERVICE FACTLET. Returns a time series of a formula/item over a &dates=start:end range (colon syntax - the web form, NOT the Excel comma-inside-parens form). Reference only.

endpoint_corporate_actions_dividends.label: Factlet: CorporateActionsDividends (web service)
endpoint_corporate_actions_dividends.notes: WEB-SERVICE FACTLET returning dividend corporate actions (ex-date, pay/record dates, amount, gross/net G or N marker, frequency, employee-bonus-share adjustment). The manual describes these as report columns rather than discrete =FDS() field codes; to pull dividend dates into Excel, verify the corresponding P_DIV* / dividend field codes and entitlement in FactSet.

endpoint_corporate_actions_splits.label: Factlet: CorporateActionsSplits (web service)
endpoint_corporate_actions_splits.notes: WEB-SERVICE FACTLET returning stock-split corporate actions (split date, split ratio/factor). Described as report columns; verify the discrete split field codes for =FDS() use.

endpoint_extract_benchmark_detail.label: Factlet: ExtractBenchmarkDetail (web service)
endpoint_extract_benchmark_detail.notes: WEB-SERVICE FACTLET returning benchmark/index constituents and their weights. For Excel use, the app already carries index weight from the universe upload; constituent weight in =FDS() is via FG_INDEX_WEIGHT("<benchmark>",0D) (see the price-series dictionary) - verify entitlement.

endpoint_extract_screen_universe.label: Factlet: ExtractScreenUniverse (web service)
endpoint_extract_screen_universe.notes: WEB-SERVICE FACTLET that runs an FSL (Screening Language) expression to return a universe of identifiers. Reference - the app's universe is supplied by upload.

endpoint_extract_vector_formula.label: Factlet: ExtractVectorFormula (web service)
endpoint_extract_vector_formula.notes: WEB-SERVICE FACTLET returning vector (array) formula output. Reference only.

endpoint_estimates_ondemand.label: Factlet: EstimatesOnDemand (web service)
endpoint_estimates_ondemand.notes: WEB-SERVICE FACTLET delivering the estimates reports (Actuals, Broker Snapshot, Consensus, Guidance, Surprise, Consensus Recommendation, Detailed Recommendation). The FE_* fields above are the reusable estimate field codes carried by these reports; the factlet is the HTTP delivery mechanism.

endpoint_lsd_ownership.label: Factlet: LSD_Ownership (web service)
endpoint_lsd_ownership.notes: WEB-SERVICE FACTLET returning ownership (holders) data. The manual documents this as a report; verify discrete ownership field codes/entitlement for any Excel use.
